The delivery_plan.json files are large and partially truncated, so exact order-level verification is limited. No agent timeout or execution errors were observed.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is marginally more effective than wo_skills for this task. Both runs produced structurally valid plans with correct channel assignments, policy compliance, and cost projections. wi_skills achieves a lower blended cost ($5.30 vs $7.13) through more aggressive use of Carrier B over the owned fleet, while wo_skills loads the owned fleet to a slightly higher 70 stops. Both runs fail JE5 (SLA-tier-differentiated ETA windows), JE6 (quantitative per-channel on-time and first-attempt success rate projections), and JE7 (channel-differentiated customer notification strategy). The skill provided structured optimization logic (density maximization, cost benchmarking) that produced a more cost-optimal result, but neither run achieved the higher-quality service-level projection or notification differentiation expected by the evaluation criteria. The difference is modest — both runs are competent and policy-compliant, but wi_skills edges ahead on cost optimization depth.
